title: Applying NLP to Automate Knowledge Discovery in Academic Research
motivation: I want to explore how large language models can accelerate literature review and hypothesis generation in multidisciplinary research.

title: Sustainable Supply Chain Strategies for Swiss Food Retail
Exploring Behavioral Nudges for Sustainable Consumer Choices
motivation: Having studied behavioral economics, I believe nudge theory can measurably shift consumer behavior toward sustainable products.

title: Applying Federated Learning to Telecom Network Optimization
description: Investigating privacy-preserving machine learning approaches for mobile network resource allocation at Swisscom.
motivation: I am fascinated by the challenge of training ML models without centralizing sensitive user data and see Swiss telecom as the ideal testing ground.

title: Digital Health Pipeline for Wearable-Based Clinical Endpoints
description: Building a data ingestion and analysis pipeline for consumer wearable data to support decentralized clinical trials at Roche.
motivation: My background in data science and interest in healthcare make this the perfect intersection of my skills and career goals.

title: Climate Risk Assessment Using Satellite and IoT Data for Reinsurance
description: Augmenting traditional actuarial catastrophe models with alternative data sources including satellite imagery and IoT sensor networks at Swiss Re.
motivation: I want to bridge my finance background with environmental data science to address the growing challenge of climate risk pricing.

title: Privacy-Preserving Health Data Analytics Framework
description: Designing a differential privacy framework for secure sharing of patient records, with deployment guidelines for Swiss hospitals.
motivation: After working on a cryptography semester project, I want to apply privacy-enhancing technologies to the sensitive domain of health data.

title: Predictive Maintenance System for SBB Train Bogies
description: Building an anomaly detection model using IoT sensor telemetry from SBB rolling stock to predict bogie failures before they occur. Currently evaluating isolation forests, autoencoders, and transformer-based approaches on historical maintenance logs.
motivation: Combining my data engineering skills with Switzerland's world-class rail infrastructure is a dream thesis opportunity.

title: Multilingual Sentiment Analysis for Swiss Public Discourse
description: Developing a transformer-based sentiment analysis system for German, French, Italian, and Romansh text from Swiss news and social media. Currently fine-tuning multilingual models and building the evaluation dataset.
motivation: As a multilingual person in a multilingual country, I want to build NLP tools that truly serve Switzerland's linguistic diversity.

title: Generative Models for Lead Optimization in Oncology
description: Comparing variational autoencoders, diffusion models, and RL-based approaches for generating molecules with improved binding affinity and drug-like properties. Working with Novartis's proprietary compound library and docking simulations.
motivation: My PhD background in computational biology positions me well to push the boundaries of AI-driven drug discovery.

title: Battery Storage Optimization for Alpine Microgrids
description: Modeling and optimizing battery energy storage sizing for off-grid Alpine communities using mixed-integer linear programming. Simulating year-round scenarios with solar, small hydro, and wind inputs under varying demand profiles.
motivation: Growing up in a rural Alpine community, I have seen first-hand the energy challenges that motivate this research.

title: Knowledge Graphs for Scientific Literature Discovery
description: Built an automated NLP pipeline that extracts entities and relations from biomedical publications to construct a domain-specific knowledge graph. Evaluated the pipeline against expert-curated benchmarks and achieved a 78% F1-score on relation extraction.
motivation: I wanted to combine my informatics skills with the challenge of making scientific knowledge more accessible and interconnected.

title: Digital Twin Prototype for Collaborative Robot Work Cells
description: Developed a simulation-based digital twin of an ABB collaborative robot work cell that ingests real-time sensor data for calibration and predictive maintenance scheduling. The prototype demonstrated a 22% improvement in maintenance prediction accuracy over schedule-based baselines.
motivation: Industrial robotics and simulation are my core interests, and this project let me combine both in a real-world setting.

title: BIM-Integrated IoT Monitoring for Construction Sites
description: Exploring the integration of IoT sensor data with BIM models for automated construction progress tracking at Hilti.
motivation: I applied because this topic perfectly matches my software development skills with my interest in construction technology.

title: Recommendation Engine for Migros Omnichannel Retail
description: Proposing to build a hybrid recommendation system for Migros's online and in-store channels.
motivation: My experience in digital commerce and data science makes me well-suited to tackle this real-world retail challenge.

title: Employer Branding ROI Measurement in Swiss Life Sciences
description: Analyzing the effectiveness of employer branding campaigns for Novartis using recruitment funnel data and A/B testing on candidate messaging across LinkedIn, Glassdoor and university channels.
motivation: I have always been fascinated by how companies attract top talent and want to bring data-driven rigor to employer branding decisions.

title: Deep Learning for Adaptive Hearing Aid Algorithms
description: Training a CNN-LSTM hybrid model on Sonova's audiometric and environmental sound datasets to dynamically adjust hearing aid parameters. Preliminary results show 18% improvement in speech intelligibility scores in noisy environments.
motivation: My grandfather's struggle with hearing aids made me realize how much technology can improve quality of life for millions.

title: Circular Economy Assessment for Consumer Electronics Plastics
description: Conducting lifecycle assessment comparing post-consumer recycled plastics versus virgin materials in Logitech mice and keyboards. Quantifying carbon, water, and toxicity impacts across the value chain.
motivation: Sustainability in tech is not just about energy efficiency — materials matter enormously and this LCA will prove it.

title: Parametric Insurance for Swiss Alpine Agriculture
description: Designing a weather-index-based parametric insurance product for Swiss Alpine farmers facing frost and drought risk. Modeling payout triggers using MeteoSwiss historical data and ERA5 reanalysis.
motivation: Insurance innovation can be a powerful climate adaptation tool, especially for vulnerable rural communities.

title: Blockchain-Based Supply Chain Traceability for Food Processing
description: Built a proof-of-concept blockchain platform for tracing grain from Swiss farms through Bühler processing equipment to packaged products. Evaluated Hyperledger Fabric vs Ethereum L2 for throughput and cost. Final prototype achieved 3-second trace queries across 12 supply chain nodes.
motivation: Food transparency is a growing consumer demand and I wanted to prove it can be achieved at industrial scale.

title: InsurTech Adoption Barriers: A Behavioral Experiment
description: Running a large-scale survey (n=1200) and behavioral experiment with Swiss insurance customers to identify trust barriers and test nudge interventions for digital insurance adoption.
motivation: Switzerland lags behind in digital insurance adoption despite being a fintech hub — I want to understand why.

title: Edge AI for Visual Quality Inspection in Manufacturing
description: Benchmarking MobileNet, EfficientNet, and YOLO variants on ABB's industrial edge hardware for real-time defect detection in metal parts. Achieving 97.2% accuracy at 45fps on a Jetson AGX Orin.
motivation: Bringing AI to the factory floor requires overcoming real engineering constraints — that is the challenge I love.

title: Cognitive Effects of Screen Time on Adolescent Decision-Making
description: Longitudinal study tracking 150 Swiss adolescents over 6 months, correlating smartphone usage patterns with cognitive performance on decision-making tasks using ecological momentary assessment.
motivation: As a developmental psychologist, I want to move beyond alarmist narratives and produce rigorous evidence on screen time effects.

title: Carbon Footprint Optimization Model for Swiss Rail Freight
description: Developing a multi-objective optimization model that minimizes carbon emissions while maintaining delivery schedules for SBB's freight operations across 1,200km of electrified track.
motivation: Rail freight is already greener than road — but I believe we can push it further with smart routing and scheduling.

title: Customer Journey Analytics for Zurich Insurance Retail
description: Building a customer segmentation engine using transactional, behavioral, and web analytics data to predict churn and identify cross-sell moments across Zurich Insurance's Swiss retail portfolio.
motivation: Insurance is a data-rich but insight-poor industry — modern analytics can transform how insurers serve customers.
